headline: Fluid Lending discovery incomplete: core Liquidity contract identified on Ethereum, audit sources located, GitHub repo referenced but contract enumeration blocked
short_headline: Fluid Lending addresses not fully enumerated

findings.text: Etherscan name-tag search: identified Fluid Liquidity proxy at 0x52Aa899454998Be5b000Ad077a46Bbe360F4e497 on Ethereum with EIP-1967 transparent proxy pattern; search for Fluid lending, factory contracts on other chains (Arbitrum, Base, Polygon) not completed

Official deployments doc located at GitHub repo (https://github.com/Instadapp/fluid-contracts-public/blob/main/deployments/deployments.md) but raw fetch failed; web search returned JSON snippets showing multiple vaults and implementations but not comprehensive per-chain enumeration
Audit PDFs identified: PeckShield, StateMind (2 audits), MixBytes (3 audits: Vault, Dex, Liquidity), Cantina (Dex); PDFs not fetched to extract in-scope addresses

GitHub repo https://github.com/Instadapp/fluid-contracts-public confirmed public; deployment scripts reference Foundry broadcast and hardhat-deploy artifacts but commit SHA not pinned and artifacts not accessed
Multi-chain declared: Ethereum, Arbitrum, Plasma, Base, Polygon; only Ethereum addresses verified via Etherscan; per-chain enumeration incomplete
Factory pattern inferred from docs (LendingFactory, VaultFactory, DexFactory) but no factory-deployed children enumerated
Only 1 verified address (Liquidity proxy) traced to fetched evidence; remaining addresses from search snippet require re-verification

rationale.verdict: Discovery blocked by inability to fetch GitHub raw deployments.md and audit PDFs within budget. One core address (Liquidity) verified on Ethereum; remaining 4 chains and factory-deployed contracts not enumerated. Grade set to unknown pending full address surface recovery.

evidence.shows: Fluid Liquidity proxy (EIP-1967 transparent proxy) on Ethereum; $1.27B TVL across 6 chains; verified contract
Audit page lists: PeckShield Pre-launch, StateMind, MixBytes Vault, Cantina Dex, MixBytes Dex, MixBytes Liquidity, StateMind Liquidity Updates audits with PDF links
Documentation references deployed contract addresses file in public GitHub repo; points to https://github.com/instadapp/fluid-contracts-public/blob/main/deployments/deployments.md
Public source repository for Fluid contracts; Liquidity layer at core with fToken and Vault protocols built on top

unknowns: D1: Arbitrum, Base, Polygon, Plasma chain explorer name-tag searches not performed
D2: GitHub deployments.md raw fetch blocked; JSON snippets from search show vault configs but cannot enumerate all contracts without full document access
D3: Audit PDF files (6 total) not fetched; in-scope contract addresses not extracted
D4-offchain: Foundry broadcast and hardhat-deploy artifacts not accessed; deployment commit SHA not pinned
D5: Cross-chain per-chain enumeration incomplete; only Ethereum verified
D6: Factory-discovered children not enumerated; LendingFactory, VaultFactory, DexFactory references in docs but no allTokens() / allVaults() calls performed
D8: Addresses from web search snippets (e.g., factory at 0x324c5Dc1fC42c7a4D43d92df1eBA58a54d13Bf2d) require block-explorer re-verification

protocol_metadata.about: Fluid is a DeFi protocol combining a Liquidity layer at its core with Lending (fToken), Vault, and DEX protocols built on top. Users supply and borrow funds through fTokens (ERC4626-compliant lending receipts) and collateralized vaults with up to 97% liquidation thresholds.
